steps.title: Identify which nouns require possessive forms
steps.content: The passage names "Racetrack Playa" (singular) and describes "162 rocks" (plural). The sentence must form two possessions: the sediment of the playa and the migration of the rocks.

steps.title: Apply the possessive rule and eliminate the distractors
steps.content:
Singular nouns form the possessive with 's; plural nouns with s' (apostrophe only).
  • A (playas sediment mark the rock's): uses non-possessive plural "playas" instead of singular possessive "playa's," and misplaces the possessive on singular "rock's" rather than plural "rocks'."
  • B (playa's sediment mark the rocks): correctly uses "playa's" but omits the possessive on "rocks," so the trails do not belong to anything.
  • C (playa's sediment mark the rocks'): correctly pairs singular possessive "playa's" with plural possessive "rocks'."
  • D (playas' sediment mark the rocks'): incorrectly uses plural possessive "playas'" for the one playa.
That confirms C is correct.
